steps.title: Use the median shortcut
steps.content: There are $5$ gas prices, so the median is the $3$rd value after the prices are put in order. You do not need to find the mean.

steps.title: Sort the prices
steps.content: Written from least to greatest, the prices are $$3.609,\ 3.679,\ 3.699,\ 3.729,\ 3.729$$ The middle value is the third number, $3.699$. In Desmos, the row $\operatorname{median}(l_1)$ is just a quick check of the same result.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A, $3.679$, is the second value, not the middle value.
  • Choice B, $3.689$, is close to the mean, but the question asks for the median.
  • Choice C, $3.699$, is the third and middle value. Correct.
  • Choice D, $3.729$, is the mode and maximum value, not the median.
That matches choice C.
